Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails (query: "advisory board" or "portfolio summary" or "client portfolio" or similar) to find David's portfolio summary email. Find email from david.chen@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com (email_id: email_david_elena_portfolio_summary_042426, subject: "Client Portfolio Summary for Advisory Board", sent April 24) UNIX Timestamp - 1777000800. The email covers 5 clients: Vectral Systems ("renewal on track, no action needed"), NorthWind Technologies ("manageable, stable, Catalina is handling"), BrightLoop Analytics ("expanding, scoping capacity"), Mosaic Robotics ("contained, insurance handling it"), and StormCloud Analytics ("resolved, stabilized"). David characterizes the portfolio as "in solid shape."
OE 2: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral") to find the Vectral Systems company record. Then search deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Vectral" or company_id: "company_vectral"). Find the Vectral Q3 renewal deal at stage "at_risk" contradicting David's claim that renewal is "on track." Also check engagement history using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_vectral"]). Find Mina's stale engagement note (engagement_vectral_board_wait_042226) from April 25 saying "still no word from Rachel Whitfield after the April 23 board meeting" which is outdated because Rachel already emailed Elena directly on April 25.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "board decision" or similar) to find Rachel's direct email to Elena. Find email from rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com (email_id: email_rachel_elena_board_decision_042526, subject: "Vectral Renewal — Board Decision and Contract Condition", sent April 25). Rachel's board approved the renewal conditionally with a performance exit clause at the 6-month mark: (1) all relocation timelines met within 48 hours of committed dates, (2) zero repeat incidents of the expense tool type, (3) a dedicated coordinator assigned to Vectral within 30 days of signing. If any benchmark is missed, Vectral can terminate with 30 days notice and no penalty. Rachel requests a 30-minute call with Elena (available Thursday PM or Friday AM which falls on April 30/May 1, after the Wednesday April 29 board session). This directly contradicts David's "straightforward approval" and "no action needed" claims.
OE 4: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Northwind" or "NorthWind") and then crm_search_deals (dealname: "NorthWind" or company_id: "company_northwind") to fact-check NorthWind Technologies. Find CRM deal "NorthWind Technologies Account Retention (Save-or-Lose)" (id: deal_northwind_retention_save_042426) at stage "at_risk" with close date April 26. Description reveals: Pam Kowalski issued formal final warning directly to David Chen on April 24, bypassing Catalina Dubois entirely. NorthWind is in active conversations with PathFinder Logistics. Retention proposal demanded by EOD Friday April 24 (note: CRM description literally states "Apr 25" but April 25, 2026 is Saturday; the intended deadline is EOD Friday April 24). This contradicts David's claim that NorthWind is "manageable" and "stable."
OE 5: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "PathFinder" or similar) to find internal context on the NorthWind crisis. Find David Chen's message in #executive (channel C004, ts 1777171200, April 26) stating "Just got a direct escalation from Pam. She bypassed Catalina entirely," naming both Raj Patel's chain-of-custody breach and Emilia Cruz's piano damage, calling out the missed April 14 recovery-plan promise, and reporting that NorthWind has already had preliminary conversations with PathFinder. Also find David's message in #customer-engagement (channel C002, ts 1777181700, April 26) declaring NorthWind a save-or-lose account and confirming the April 14 recovery plan was never delivered to Pam. Additionally find Catalina Dubois's earlier message in #operations (channel C006, ts 1776168600, April 14) admitting she is overwhelmed and falling behind on NorthWind follow-ups, with Raj Patel's chain-of-custody fallout and Emilia Cruz's piano damage both still open. This contradicts David's claim that "Catalina is handling the relationship and has communicated a recovery plan."
OE 6: Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "NorthWind") to verify NorthWind relocation records. Find Emilia Cruz relocation record (recReloc_northwind_cruz_updated) showing Steinway Model B piano scratched during extraction, $1,200 insurance claim filed with KeyMove (status: PENDING), Blessing Okafor admitted walkup assessment underestimated stairwell turn radius, and Julian logged an unanswered callback complaint from Emilia on April 12. Also find Raj Patel relocation record. This confirms the damage was not "minor" as David characterized it.
OE 7: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop") and crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_brightloop"), search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "BrightLoop" or similar), and search Airtable using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "BrightLoop") to fact-check BrightLoop Analytics. Find in email and Slack that the April batch had unresolved service failures David omitted: Simone Richter placed in studio instead of promised 1BR (Mina never followed up after April 9), and Marcus Webb car shipment delayed with two unanswered follow-ups. Find in Airtable Oliver Bancroft's new relocation record (recReloc_brightloop_bancroft_may) showing London to Boston MoveOps' first international relocation with NO coordinator assigned, pending Elena's approval of a temp coordinator hire that Chloe and David escalated on April 24 with no response.
OE 8: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic") and crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_mosaic"), search emails using search_emails (query: "Mosaic" or "prototype" or "damage" or similar), and search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mosaic" or "prototype damage" or similar) to fact-check Mosaic Robotics. Find the $90,000 prototype damage incident with a $40,000 uncovered insurance gap (MoveOps' vendor liability insurance covers only $50,000 per item, leaving a $40,000 gap on the $90,000 unit value). Blessing Okafor signed off on packing remotely without verifying foam density. Delivery was accepted by an intern, not the authorized recipient. Rafael Mendes (rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com, CC'd on formal damage correspondence from Tamara Byrd) has executive visibility. This contradicts David's claim that the situation is "contained" and "handled through insurance."
OE 9: Search emails using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Liam Park" or "outstanding items" or similar) to fact-check StormCloud Analytics. Find email from liam.park@stormcloud.io to elena.rostova@moveops.com (email_id: email_liam_elena_stormcloud_followup_042526, subject: "Re: Outstanding Items — StormCloud Account", sent April 25). Liam lists 3 unresolved issues: (1) Jae-won Kim's Austin lease remains unsigned, landlord threatening to release unit May 1, Jae-won in hotel at own expense since April 14, Liam states Airtable shows "Completed" (actual record Status is "In Progress" with a Special Requirements note flagging the previous incorrect Completed status); (2) Simone Richter's standing desk stipend frozen since March under Marcus Thorne's blanket hold (the exact $340 amount is discoverable via Simone's original email to Mina, email_id: email_email_4df93f95c584, where she asks about her $340 standing desk claim submitted March 25); (3) Ridgeline Robotics referral dropped Kenji Watanabe followed up twice with no MoveOps response. Liam threatens to evaluate alternatives for Q3. This directly contradicts David's claim that StormCloud is "resolved" and "stabilized."
OE 10: Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Jae-won Kim") to verify the stale relocation record. Find record (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) with Status "In Progress" but Special Requirements noting "Record incorrectly shows Completed" and "Lease still unsigned." The lease is actually unsigned, the landlord is threatening to release the unit May 1, and Jae-won has been in a hotel at his own expense since April 14. The Special Requirements field needs updating with the full housing crisis details.
OE 11: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Terraform" or "Vantage") to find Terraform Digital's company record (company_id: "company-terraform-digital"), then crm_search_deals (company_id: "company-terraform-digital") to surface deals. Also search crm_search_deals (dealname: "Canopy" or similar) to surface other clients David omitted entirely. Find Terraform Digital deal (CRM company name: "Vantage Distributed", company_id: "company-terraform-digital") MoveOps' largest client ($1.85M enterprise relocation contract per deal-terraform-digital-001, over 1,000 employees and contractors), first relocation batch of 3 employees (deal_id: "deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1", amount: $7,500) in qualification stage. Find Canopy Health executive relocation deal for Dr. Anand Mehta (CMO, Boston to Chicago, $12,170, deal_id: "crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb") MoveOps' first C-suite placement. Both are strategically significant accounts Elena should know about before the board session.
OE 12: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "pricing exposure" or similar) to surface the omitted Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice cross-swap. Find Emeka Diallo's message in #customer-engagement (channel C002, ts 1776946980, April 23) where he reconstructs the invoice issue: QB records were correct (Sunbelt billed on INV-2026-0398 at $4,800, Palmetto on INV-2026-0399 at $2,400) but the April 10 email send crossed the PDF attachments so each client received the other's document. Also find Emeka's follow-up message in the same channel (channel C002, ts 1777078800, April 25) addressed directly to David, revealing that Raj Greenfield (Sunbelt) called and now knows Palmetto pays half his rate — he asked point-blank whether MoveOps has a standard rate. Carmen at Palmetto needs a corrected invoice immediately for nonprofit audit. Emeka notes this is the second pricing exposure this quarter and that Elena does not know about it yet. David never included this in his summary to Elena.
OE 13: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "vendor" or "margin" or "Heartland" or "Swift" or "rate increase" or similar) to surface the omitted vendor cost crisis. Find Alejandro Fuentes's message in #finance (channel C005, ts 1776280500, April 15) detailing exact vendor rate increases: Heartland +20% starting Q3 and Swift +15% on new bookings effective immediately plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment. Also find Marcus Thorne's message in #executive (channel C004, ts 1777164600, April 26) revealing these combined rate increases push three accounts below the 25% margin floor (Mosaic, Palmetto, BrightLoop), noting $1,200–$1,800 in additional Heartland billing discrepancies beyond the original $4,800 dispute, and flagging that the temp coordinator hire decision for BrightLoop is still pending with no response from Elena. David omitted this entirely.
OE 14: Check Elena's calendar availability using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: "2026-04-27 00:00:00", end_datetime: "2026-04-29 23:59:59") to find open slots on Monday April 27 and Tuesday April 28 for scheduling the pre-Wednesday meetings.
OE 15: Update Airtable Client Account record for NorthWind Technologies using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblClientAccts01", records: [{id: "recAcct000000064", fields: {...}}]). Note: the Status field has no "At Risk" option, so risk context must go into Notes. Notes updated with Pam Kowalski formal final warning (April 24), PathFinder competitor evaluation, save-or-lose retention status, EOD Friday April 24 retention proposal deadline passed.
OE 16: Update Airtable Client Account record for StormCloud Analytics using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblClientAccts01", records: [{id: "recAcct000000063", fields: {...}}]). Notes updated with Liam Park direct escalation to Elena (April 25), Jae-won Kim housing crisis, Simone Richter $340 stipend frozen under blanket hold, dropped Ridgeline Robotics referral, Liam threatening to evaluate alternatives for Q3.
OE 17: Update Airtable Client Account record for BrightLoop Analytics using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblClientAccts01", records: [{id: "recAcct000000005", fields: {...}}]). Notes updated with April service failures (Simone Richter studio-vs-1BR, Marcus Webb delayed car shipment), May expansion wave including Oliver Bancroft international move with no coordinator assigned, temp coordinator hire pending Elena approval.
OE 18: Update Airtable Client Account record for Mosaic Robotics using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblClientAccts01", records: [{id: "recAcct000000006", fields: {...}}]). Status currently "Onboarding." Notes updated with $90K prototype damage, $40K uncovered insurance gap, Blessing Okafor remote sign-off failure, delivery accepted by intern, Rafael Mendes executive visibility.
OE 19: Update Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{id: "recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin", fields: {...}}]) to correct the Jae-won Kim record: Special Requirements updated with full housing crisis details lease unsigned, UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork, Jae-won in hotel at own expense since April 14, landlord threatening to release unit May 1. The record's Status field currently shows "In Progress" with a note flagging that it previously incorrectly showed "Completed" (as Liam Park noted in his email to Elena).
OE 20: Update the CRM Vectral renewal deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage (id: "deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk", dealstage: "contractsent") to reflect that Vectral's board voted to approve the renewal conditionally the deal has progressed past "at_risk" since the board approved terms, but contract finalization is pending due to the performance exit clause conditions that require CEO-level discussion before signing.
OE 21: Schedule a meeting between Elena and Rachel Whitfield using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: relating to Vectral Renewal Conditions Discussion or similar, start_datetime/end_datetime: 30-minute slot on Monday April 27 or Tuesday April 28 before the Wednesday April 29 board session, attendees: ["elena.rostova@moveops.com", "rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com"], description: referencing the board-approved performance exit clause with 3 benchmarks that need CEO-level discussion before the advisory board session). Note: Rachel's email offered Thursday PM or Friday AM (April 30/May 1), which is after the board session the agent should propose earlier times to Rachel given the urgency, or send a reply/email to Rachel requesting availability on Monday or Tuesday instead.
OE 22: Schedule a NorthWind retention emergency meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: relating to NorthWind Retention Briefing or similar, start_datetime/end_datetime: slot before Wednesday, attendees: must include elena.rostova@moveops.com and may include david.chen@moveops.com and/or catalina.dubois@moveops.com, description: referencing save-or-lose status, Pam Kowalski's formal final warning, PathFinder competitor evaluation, need for Elena to approve retention strategy and pricing concessions).
OE 23: Schedule a StormCloud response preparation meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: relating to StormCloud / Liam Park Response or similar, start_datetime/end_datetime: slot before Wednesday, attendees: must include elena.rostova@moveops.com, description: referencing Liam Park's direct email to Elena with 3 unresolved issues and his threat to evaluate alternatives for Q3).
OE 24: Deliver a comprehensive fact-check briefing to the user (Priya Chakrabarti). The agent may send an email using send_email (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com or priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com, recipients: ["elena.rostova@moveops.com"], subject: relating to David's Portfolio Summary Fact-Check or similar) or deliver findings directly in the final response to the user. Content must cover: what David said vs. what the systems actually show for each of the 5 clients Vectral (conditional approval with performance exit clause, not "straightforward"), NorthWind (save-or-lose with formal final warning, not "manageable"), BrightLoop (April service failures and unscoped international expansion, not just "scoping capacity"), Mosaic ($90K damage with $40K uncovered gap, not "contained"), StormCloud (3 unresolved issues per Liam's email, not "resolved") plus clients David omitted entirely (Terraform Digital $1.85M contract, Canopy Health first C-suite relocation), issues David omitted (Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice cross-swap, vendor cost crisis with margin compression), and a summary of records updated and meetings scheduled.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails (query: "advisory board" or "portfolio summary" or "client portfolio" or similar) to find David's portfolio summary email. Find email from david.chen@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com (email_id: email_david_elena_portfolio_summary_042426, subject: "Client Portfolio Summary for Advisory Board", sent April 24) UNIX Timestamp - 1777000800. The email covers 5 clients: Vectral Systems ("renewal on track, no action needed"), NorthWind Technologies ("manageable, stable, Catalina is handling"), BrightLoop Analytics ("expanding, scoping capacity"), Mosaic Robotics ("contained, insurance handling it"), and StormCloud Analytics ("resolved, stabilized"). David characterizes the portfolio as "in solid shape."
OE 2: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral") to find the Vectral Systems company record. Then search deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Vectral" or company_id: "company_vectral"). OE 3: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "board decision" or similar) to find Rachel's direct email to Elena. Rachel's board approved the renewal conditionally with a performance exit clause at the 6-month mark: (1) all relocation timelines met within 48 hours of committed dates, (2) zero repeat incidents of the expense tool type, (3) a dedicated coordinator assigned to Vectral within 30 days of signing. If any benchmark is missed, Vectral can terminate with 30 days notice and no penalty. Rachel requests a 30-minute call with Elena (available Thursday PM or Friday AM which falls on April 30/May 1, after the Wednesday April 29 board session). This directly contradicts David's "straightforward approval" and "no action needed" claims.
OE 4: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Northwind" or "NorthWind") and then crm_search_deals (dealname: "NorthWind" or company_id: "company_northwind") to fact-check NorthWind Technologies. Find CRM deal "NorthWind Technologies Account Retention (Save-or-Lose)" (id: deal_northwind_retention_save_042426) at stage "at_risk" with close date April 26. Description reveals: Pam Kowalski issued formal final warning directly to David Chen on April 24, bypassing Catalina Dubois entirely. NorthWind is in active conversations with PathFinder Logistics. OE 5: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "PathFinder" or similar) to find internal context on the NorthWind crisis. Find David Chen's message in #executive (channel C004, ts 1777171200, April 26) stating "Just got a direct escalation from Pam. OE 6: Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "NorthWind") to verify NorthWind relocation records. Also find Raj Patel relocation record. This confirms the damage was not "minor" as David characterized it.
OE 7: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop") and crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_brightloop"), search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "BrightLoop" or similar), and search Airtable using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "BrightLoop") to fact-check BrightLoop Analytics. Find in email and Slack that the April batch had unresolved service failures David omitted: Simone Richter placed in studio instead of promised 1BR (Mina never followed up after April 9), and Marcus Webb car shipment delayed with two unanswered follow-ups. Find in Airtable Oliver Bancroft's new relocation record (recReloc_brightloop_bancroft_may) showing London to Boston MoveOps' first international relocation with NO coordinator assigned, pending Elena's approval of a temp coordinator hire that Chloe and David escalated on April 24 with no response.
OE 8: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic") and crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_mosaic"), search emails using search_emails (query: "Mosaic" or "prototype" or "damage" or similar), and search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mosaic" or "prototype damage" or similar) to fact-check Mosaic Robotics. Find the $90,000 prototype damage incident with a $40,000 uncovered insurance gap (MoveOps' vendor liability insurance covers only $50,000 per item, leaving a $40,000 gap on the $90,000 unit value). Blessing Okafor signed off on packing remotely without verifying foam density. Delivery was accepted by an intern, not the authorized recipient. Rafael Mendes (rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com, CC'd on formal damage correspondence from Tamara Byrd) has executive visibility. This contradicts David's claim that the situation is "contained" and "handled through insurance."
OE 9: Search emails using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Liam Park" or "outstanding items" or similar) to fact-check StormCloud Analytics. Find email from liam.park@stormcloud.io to elena.rostova@moveops.com (email_id: email_liam_elena_stormcloud_followup_042526, subject: "Re: Outstanding Items — StormCloud Account", sent April 25). Liam lists 3 unresolved issues: (1) Jae-won Kim's Austin lease remains unsigned, landlord threatening to release unit May 1, Jae-won in hotel at own expense since April 14, Liam states Airtable shows "Completed" (actual record Status is "In Progress" with a Special Requirements note flagging the previous incorrect Completed status); (2) Simone Richter's standing desk stipend frozen since March under Marcus Thorne's blanket hold (the exact $340 amount is discoverable via Simone's original email to Mina, email_id: email_email_4df93f95c584, where she asks about her $340 standing desk claim submitted March 25); (3) Ridgeline Robotics referral dropped Kenji Watanabe followed up twice with no MoveOps response. Liam threatens to evaluate alternatives for Q3. This directly contradicts David's claim that StormCloud is "resolved" and "stabilized."
OE 10: Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Jae-won Kim") to verify the stale relocation record. Find record (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) with Status "In Progress" but Special Requirements noting "Record incorrectly shows Completed" and "Lease still unsigned." The lease is actually unsigned, the landlord is threatening to release the unit May 1, and Jae-won has been in a hotel at his own expense since April 14. The Special Requirements field needs updating with the full housing crisis details.
OE 11: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Terraform" or "Vantage") to find Terraform Digital's company record (company_id: "company-terraform-digital"), then crm_search_deals (company_id: "company-terraform-digital") to surface deals. Also search crm_search_deals (dealname: "Canopy" or similar) to surface other clients David omitted entirely. Find Terraform Digital deal (CRM company name: "Vantage Distributed", company_id: "company-terraform-digital") MoveOps' largest client ($1.85M enterprise relocation contract per deal-terraform-digital-001, over 1,000 employees and contractors), first relocation batch of 3 employees (deal_id: "deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1", amount: $7,500) in qualification stage. Find Canopy Health executive relocation deal for Dr. Anand Mehta (CMO, Boston to Chicago, $12,170, deal_id: "crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb") MoveOps' first C-suite placement. Both are strategically significant accounts Elena should know about before the board session.
OE 12: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "pricing exposure" or similar) to surface the omitted Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice cross-swap. Find Emeka Diallo's message in #customer-engagement (channel C002, ts 1776946980, April 23) where he reconstructs the invoice issue: QB records were correct (Sunbelt billed on INV-2026-0398 at $4,800, Palmetto on INV-2026-0399 at $2,400) but the April 10 email send crossed the PDF attachments so each client received the other's document. Also find Emeka's follow-up message in the same channel (channel C002, ts 1777078800, April 25) addressed directly to David, revealing that Raj Greenfield (Sunbelt) called and now knows Palmetto pays half his rate — he asked point-blank whether MoveOps has a standard rate. Carmen at Palmetto needs a corrected invoice immediately for nonprofit audit. Emeka notes this is the second pricing exposure this quarter and that Elena does not know about it yet. David never included this in his summary to Elena.
OE 13: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "vendor" or "margin" or "Heartland" or "Swift" or "rate increase" or similar) to surface the omitted vendor cost crisis. Find Alejandro Fuentes's message in #finance (channel C005, ts 1776280500, April 15) detailing exact vendor rate increases: Heartland +20% starting Q3 and Swift +15% on new bookings effective immediately plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment. Also find Marcus Thorne's message in #executive (channel C004, ts 1777164600, April 26) revealing these combined rate increases push three accounts below the 25% margin floor (Mosaic, Palmetto, BrightLoop), noting $1,200–$1,800 in additional Heartland billing discrepancies beyond the original $4,800 dispute, and flagging that the temp coordinator hire decision for BrightLoop is still pending with no response from Elena. David omitted this entirely.
OE 14: Check Elena's calendar availability using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: "2026-04-27 00:00:00", end_datetime: "2026-04-29 23:59:59") to find open slots on Monday April 27 and Tuesday April 28 for scheduling the pre-Wednesday meetings.

Note: Rachel's email offered Thursday PM or Friday AM (April 30/May 1), which is after the board session the agent should propose earlier times to Rachel given the urgency, or send a reply/email to Rachel requesting availability on Monday or Tuesday instead.

OE 24: Deliver a comprehensive fact-check briefing to the user (Priya Chakrabarti). The agent may send an email using send_email (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com or priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com, recipients: ["elena.rostova@moveops.com"], subject: relating to David's Portfolio Summary Fact-Check or similar) or deliver findings directly in the final response to the user. Content must cover: what David said vs. what the systems actually show for each of the 5 clients Vectral (conditional approval with performance exit clause, not "straightforward"), NorthWind (save-or-lose with formal final warning, not "manageable"), BrightLoop (April service failures and unscoped international expansion, not just "scoping capacity"), Mosaic ($90K damage with $40K uncovered gap, not "contained"), StormCloud (3 unresolved issues per Liam's email, not "resolved") plus clients David omitted entirely (Terraform Digital $1.85M contract, Canopy Health first C-suite relocation), issues David omitted (Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice cross-swap, vendor cost crisis with margin compression), and a summary of records updated and meetings scheduled.
